question_text: Two runners start at the same point. Runner A maintains a constant speed of 8 m/s for 60 seconds. Runner B accelerates uniformly from rest at 0.2 m/s² for the same 60 seconds. Using a speed-time graph, determine who has travelled further and by how much.
correct_answer: Runner A has travelled further by 120 metres. Runner A: 8 × 60 = 480 m. Runner B: 0.5 × 0.2 × 60² = 360 m. Difference = 120 m.
explanation: On a speed-time graph, distance is the area under the line. Runner A's graph is a horizontal rectangle (area = base × height = 60 × 8 = 480 m), while Runner B's is a triangle (area = 0.5 × base × height = 0.5 × 60 × 12 = 360 m, since final velocity = 0.2 × 60 = 12 m/s). Runner A travels 120 m further.
